title: Random Forests, Tree by Tree
tagline: Bagging + random feature sampling — why a forest of weak trees beats a strong one
summary: Watch trees enter a forest one at a time on a 2-D classification problem; the boundary smooths out, the out-of-bag error converges, feature-importance bars stabilise. Compare bagging-only, RF (= bagging + random features), and Extra-Trees side-by-side; tune n_estimators and max_features and see the bias-variance trade play out live.
highlights: Click 'Add tree' and watch the forest boundary smooth tree-by-tree.
OOB error tracked live as trees enter; converges to a held-out test error you don't have to leave out.
Side-by-side bagging-only vs RF vs Extra-Trees on the same dataset.
Feature-importance bars + partial-dependence panel so 'why this prediction' has an honest answer.
